methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Transportation. Range: $270M - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
The ePlane Company is a Series B transportation-sector startup founded by Satya Chakravarthy, having raised $14M+ and currently seeking $40–50M. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 8 private companies in transportation, including Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft at comparable or adjacent stages; no public comparables are available in this nascent segment.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $14M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B step-ups, yielding $27M. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies (Einride at $1.8B Series C, Harbinger at $500M Series C, REGENT Craft at $270M Series A) produces a median of $500M, with a range of $270M–$1.8B. The wide dispersion reflects stage heterogeneity and the early-stage, high-uncertainty nature of advanced transportation ventures.

Triangulating these methods yields a range of $23M to $575M, with a median of $263M. The stage-progression floor ($27M) reflects cumulative capital deployed; the peer-median ceiling ($500M+) reflects market comparables but carries significant uncertainty due to stage mismatch, technology differentiation, and limited precedent for electric vertical-lift aircraft commercialization. Key variables affecting valuation include regulatory pathway clarity, production feasibility, and demand validation.
